mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in nonfarm payroll employment raises expected household income and aggregate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth is the signal input.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 138780 thousand
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with improving labor demand
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, the discount-rate component of equity valuation stays suppressed and investors accept higher equity multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% or remains below 0.25% for at least 3 consecutive monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 0.1 percent
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce negative mean 60-day S&P 500 forward returns or if positive return frequency is below 50% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPI while FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero lowers ex-post real policy rates but raises future tightening risk and margin pressure; the equity market reprices negatively over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above its prior 12-month pace while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 237.083
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in retail sales signals weaker nominal consumer demand and lower near-term revenue growth for listed firms, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 439891 million dollars
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion vulnerable to demand slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 3-month declines are followed by nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns and the hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates improving operating leverage and broader cyclical output growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 103.6585
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO trends do not forecast higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO trends at p < 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in housing starts below a recent trend threshold signals weaker residential investment and durable-goods demand, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average by more than 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 1001 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST shortfalls are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative-return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll gains and a falling unemployment rate identify genuine labor-market tightening rather than participation noise, supporting consumption and earnings revisions and producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 6.1 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 25 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: When real output and payroll employment both expand, aggregate demand is broad enough to sustain revenue growth; the combined growth signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly change is positive and PAYEMS latest monthly change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot available before 2014-07-15 was 15824.2 billions of chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: broad-based expansion with synchronized output and employment growth
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a 5-day S&P 500 positive-return frequency below the unconditional frequency or a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02.
